channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trudeau says Americans will pay more whenever Trump decides to impose tariffs on Canada
item.description: Outgoing Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says prices for Americans will go up if President Donald Trump follows through with his vow to apply sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world’s biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that’s home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Davos 2025: Some notable quotes from the World Economic Forum meeting
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Leading business and political figures attending the World Economic Forum meeting in Davos, Switzerland, have discussed and debated topics such as technology, tariffs, climate change, Ukraine, Gaza and the global economy this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Croatia warns against travel to Serbia after 5 civil society activists were deported
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia warned its citizens Thursday to postpone unnecessary travel to neighboring Serbia after the deportation of five Croatian activists who had taken part in a meeting of civil society organizations in the Serbian capital, Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump picks former fast-food chain CEO Andrew Puzder for EU ambassador
item.description: President Trump has selected Andrew Puzder, a former fast-food chain CEO who he attempted to install in his first White House Cabinet, as his pick for ambassador to the European Union. Trump praised Puzder, the former chief of CKE restaurants, as steering fast-food chains like Carl’s Jr.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: How the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal will unfold
item.description: Israeli troops have pulled back to the edges of Gaza, the first hostages have been released and many Palestinians have returned to what remains of their homes in the first few days of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Lee Keath, Samy Magdy And Abby Sewell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Authorities ramp up efforts to protect sea turtles as deaths on India's shores continue to mount
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Dead sea turtles keep washing ashore on India's east coast and environmentalists and authorities are trying frantically to stop the number of 600 dead from getting even higher.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Davos 2025: Trump leaves an impression with his video address to the World Economic Forum
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump drew pockets of laughter and a few moans with his blunt comments to an international audience while appearing by video link at the World Economic Forum meeting in Davos, Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy's top court upholds Amanda Knox's conviction for falsely accusing innocent man of murder
item.description: Italy’s highest court has confirmed a slander conviction against U.S. defendant Amanda Knox for accusing an innocent man in her British flatmate’s 2007 murder, a sensational case that polarized trial watchers on both sides of the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy under fire from International Criminal Court for releasing Libya warlord accused of war crimes
item.description: ROME (AP) — Human rights groups voiced outrage Wednesday after Italy released a Libyan warlord on a technicality, after he was arrested on a warrant from the International Criminal Court accusing him of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 people are killed in a knife attack in Germany; Scholz says there must be consequences
item.description: A stabbing attack in Germany has left two people dead, including a 2-year-old boy, and three others injured; the suspect was arrested shortly after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murdoch's UK tabloids apologize to Prince Harry and admit intruding on the late Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry claimed a “monumental” victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology for intruding in his life for years, and agreed to pay substantial damages to settle his privacy invasion lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama protests 'threat' of force as Trump pushed to reclaim canal
item.description: The Panamanian government formally raised concerns with the United Nations over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal, noting any threat of force would violate rules. In a letter to U.N. Secretary General António Guterres, Panama points to U.N.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and panic at Turkish ski resort as fire ripped through hotel where 79 died
item.description: KARTALKAYA, Turkey (AP) — As flames tore through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey, friends Esra Karakisa and Halime Cetin watched helplessly as the horror unfolded before them.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A rebel group is advancing on eastern Congo's largest city and over 100,000 people have fled
item.description: NZULO, Congo (AP) — The M23 rebel group's advance toward eastern Congo's largest city has displaced over 178,000 people in the past two weeks, the United Nations said, as the fighters closed in on Goma on the border with Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of missing Marine veteran calls Trump admin a 'breath of fresh air' as she continues 12-year search
item.description: Debra Tice, mother of missing Marine veteran and American journalist Austin Tice, has returned to Syria to search for her son who was taken captive in August 2012.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The State Department has suspended flights bringing previously approved refugees to the United States, cutting off access to protection in advance of the timeline set by President Trump in a new order pausing the program. An email reviewed Wednesday by The Associated Press, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: NFL legends make emotional trip to Israel in push for hostages' release
item.description: Former NFL stars Nick Lowery and Tony Richardson traveled to Israel where they met with leaders and the family of Hamas hostage Naama Levy in an effort to bring attention to the plight of the captives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: JINSAFUT, West Bank (AP) — Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank late Monday, setting cars and property ablaze, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: AREF TUFAHA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police investigate whether foreign actors are paying for antisemitic crimes in Australia
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian detectives are investigating whether foreign actors are paying criminals to commit antisemitic attacks in the country, police said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Russia sanctions ‘likely’ if Moscow doesn’t negotiate Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday it was likely he would impose additional sanctions on Russia if it did not come to the negotiating table to broker a ceasefire in Ukraine. "Sounds likely," Trump said of potential sanctions on Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 66 dead after fire erupts at popular ski hotel in Turkey
item.description: At least 66 people died and 51 others became injured in a fire that broke out at a Turkish ski resort hotel on Tuesday. Over 200 guests were staying at the hotel when the disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
